message:
test(fcp-conformance/interop/session): retire local helpers, exercise production fcp_protocol APIs (resolves br-t6wmw)

Pre-fix the session interop suite maintained a parallel set of local
helpers that DRIFTED from the production fcp_protocol::session::*
surface — most visibly:

  * Local `negotiate_suite(&[&str], &[&str])` vs production
    `negotiate_suite(&[SessionCryptoSuite], &[SessionCryptoSuite])`
    that enforces MINIMUM_SUITE floor + responder-picks invariant.
  * Local 3-field `TransportLimits { max_datagram_bytes, max_frame_bytes,
    max_symbols_per_frame }` vs production single-field `u16
    max_datagram_bytes` wrapper.
  * Local HashSet-based `is_nonce_fresh` keyed only on raw nonce bytes
    vs production `HelloReplayWindow` keyed on `(from, nonce)` so two
    distinct senders can legitimately use overlapping nonces.
  * Custom `build_retry_hello` binary format vs production
    CBOR-encoded `MeshSessionHelloRetry`.
  * Local `is_datagram_valid` vs production `FcpsDatagram::decode`
    rejecting on `bytes.len() > max_datagram_bytes`.

`session_interop_tests_pass` reported all green while never exercising
the production code paths. This commit replaces every helper with the
production API:

  test_suite_negotiation        → fcp_protocol::session::negotiate_suite
                                  + SessionCryptoSuite enum
  test_hello_retry_cookie       → MeshSessionHelloRetry construction +
                                  canonical-CBOR round-trip
  test_transport_limits_*       → production TransportLimits (single
                                  u16 field) + FcpsDatagram::decode for
                                  enforcement
  test_nonce_freshness          → HelloReplayWindow::check_and_update
                                  with real (from, nonce) keying that
                                  pins the cross-sender semantics

The two HKDF/X25519 vector tests (transcript_determinism,
session_id_binding) keep exercising their inputs directly because they
test the key-derivation transcript shape, not a session helper.

Removed: `fn negotiate_suite`, `fn negotiate_limits`,
`struct TransportLimits` (local), `fn is_datagram_valid`,
`fn is_nonce_fresh`, `fn build_retry_hello`, plus their unit-test
fan-out (the production-API equivalents are already covered by
crates/fcp-conformance/tests/session_suite_negotiation_conformance.rs
and session_nonce_replay_conformance.rs).

cargo test -p fcp-conformance: 921 lib + integration suites all green;
session_interop_via_struct sees the same 7 tests, all production-backed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
